headline: Exxon Mobil Weighs Hong Kong Esso Sale And Refocuses Capital Priorities
summary: Exxon Mobil, ticker NYSE:XOM, is reportedly exploring the sale of its Esso branded gas station network in Hong Kong. The potential divestment would mark a sizeable exit from a long standing retail presence in the city. The move is understood to be part of a broader effort to streamline Exxon Mobil's portfolio in Asia. The reported Hong Kong network sale comes at a time when Exxon Mobil shares trade around $150.53, with the stock up 22.7% year to date and 43.3% over the past year. Over...
